hero.subtitle: Scroll slowly. The path appears as you move. ↓
hero.h1: For 10 years, I took <hl1>the same journey</hl1>, looking out the <hl2>same window…</hl2>
hero.block2: and every day… <hl>I discovered something new.</hl>
hero.block3: I have a different way of looking at things. I see <hl1>past the first glance</hl1>, and that is why I know that <hl2>what is essential is invisible to the eye...</hl2>
hero.block4: I'm a <job>Product Designer</job> with <exp>4 years</exp> of experience in <skill>UX/UI</skill> and 1 year as <role>Product Manager</role>. Uncovering new ideas. Identifying opportunities. Designing with business strategy at the core.

hero.accent: Adding the secret sauce to the burger.

desafios.title: Desafíos y aprendizajes: Orden en el desorden

1.title: Product Manager sin recetas
1.body: Mi primera experiencia formal como Product Manager sin guía concreta. Mi primer roadmap ordenó el proyecto pero no al equipo. El segundo fue claro y el equipo lo implementó con autonomía.
1.badge_body: Profundizar en la implementación de roadmaps según las necesidades de cada equipo.

2.title: Sincronización de un equipo discontinuo
2.body: Sin dedicación full-time del equipo, el flujo se cortaba. Implementé un tablero Kanban en Trello con columna de prioridades estratégicas para mantener el rumbo.
2.badge_body: Implementar herramientas de gestión ágiles para facilitar el orden y la autonomía de las tareas.

3.title: Documentación desordenada
3.body: Como fue mi primera experiencia, el proceso de documentación fue desordenado y difícilmente transmisible, quedando en notas personales e informales.
3.badge_body: Refinar mi capacidad de documentar de forma clara y accionable para que cualquier persona pueda recurrir a ello.

4.title: Data Cleaning en GA4
4.body: Al compartir la URL con los clientes, los datos estaban "sucios". Tuve que aprender a filtrar métricas avanzadas para separar el ruido del tráfico real.
4.badge_body: Profundizar en Google Analytics para sistemas complejos y manejar registros más claros de datos.

5.title: Legitimar la estrategia (El Chef sin título)
5.body: Lograr que la estrategia de producto se plasmara en la comunicación de marketing implicó varias reuniones y aprender a retransmitir ideas de forma simple.
5.badge_body: Perfeccionar la comunicación asertiva para adaptar el mensaje técnico a diferentes perfiles profesionales.

6.title: Estrategia de Pricing y Business Analytics
6.body: La estrategia de monetización se definió por investigación con usuarios sin un pricing concreto. Realicé los análisis de business analytics a posteriori.
6.badge_body: Integrar investigaciones de business analytics desde la etapa de descubrimiento para definir precios correctamente.

cierre.quote: Gracias por leer...Hasta la próxima receta.

logre.title: What I achieved?
strategy_items: Strategic alignment across all areas: Marketing, Founders, and Development.

1.title: Premium plan adoption
1.body: Conversion to the paid plan was slower than expected. More effective activation strategies were needed to drive the upgrade.

2.title: User churn
2.body: The exact drop-off location within the initial conversion funnel could not be accurately measured.

3.title: Tracking active stores
3.body: Incomplete data tracking for active stores limited the accuracy of the retention analysis.

1.title: Nuevo roadmap
1.body: Creé un nuevo roadmap con el objetivo de convertir a premium los clientes free, priorizando las funciones de mayor valor percibido.

2.title: Onboarding guiado
2.body: Diseñé un onboarding guiado para que las tiendas terminen de configurarse y activen todo su potencial desde el primer día.

3.title: Estrategia de captación premium
3.body: Definí una estrategia de captación en redes sociales, comunicación directa y free trial estratégico para convertir usuarios free en premium.

4.title: Mejoras en el registro de métricas
4.body: Propuse implementar sistemas de medición más precisos para datos de actividad, retención y comportamiento de usuario.

images.before_after_alt: Landing page before and after the redesign
images.item1: Landing page redesign focused on the niche, ideal client, and conversion.
images.laptop_text: Product customization with extras, preferences, and add-ons.

process.stage1_step1_body: <hl>Discovery and research.</hl> I had in-depth conversations with the founders, researched our clients, looked for metrics, and analyzed the landing page, the platform, and social media.
process.stage1_step2_title: 2º - Whipped up some quick snacks before the big feast:
process.stage1_step2_body: Before the main course, I tackled the urgent request: I analyzed the landing page and made <hl>UX writing updates</hl> to quickly address the founders' immediate goal of acquiring more clients.
process.stage1_result_body: Sign-ups <hl>jumped from 2 to 5 monthly registrations</hl> with changes implemented in just one day. This satisfied the founders' immediate appetite while I prepared the in-depth research.
process.quote: Now, with full bellies and happy hearts, it's time to prep the main course...
process.stage2_title:
Second Stage: 
Searching for the secret ingredients: deep-dive research.
stage2_product_discoveries: The majority of the clients belonged to the food industry, primarily burger joints.
Competitors offered for free what was originally this platform's monetization model (Mercado Pago integration). They also provided features that Pedilo lacked.

There was a clear demand during interviews: they needed consumers to be able to customize their burgers by adding extras. Features that had been previously planned, such as email marketing promotions or inventory management, were of no interest.
Clients preferred paying a flat fee instead of commissions.
stage2_work_research: Analyzing the workspace board and the extensive backlog.
stage2_work_discoveries: There was no strategy in place for prioritizing features, bugs, etc.
The team worked on a part-time, discontinuous basis; as a result, whenever someone picked the product back up, they simply worked on whatever tasks they saw fit.
stage2_social_research: Analysis of what and how things were being communicated on social media.
stage2_social_discoveries: Social media communication was tailored to the end consumer instead of the ideal client.
The team did not have a clear understanding of the product; they confused the platform's features with those of food delivery apps.
Communication failed to address both the solution and the ideal client's pain points.
process.stage2_insight_body:
Competitors offered the option to customize the URL, but I discovered that our organic client acquisition happened because businesses saw the Pedilo branding on other clients' stores.
Implementing this feature just to align with the competition would have resulted in losing our primary organic client acquisition channel.
process.stage3_title:
Third Stage: 
Let's cook! Roadmap and execution
stage3_step1_items: Aligned the marketing strategy with the landing page strategy.
Defined design and development tasks and priorities.
Implemented an event correlation system, linking product milestones and marketing campaigns to fluctuations in key metrics.
stage3_step2_items: Implement the star feature that users would pay for—Order Customization: Yes, we all want to add extra cheese to our burger.
Redesign the landing page, making it 100% niche-focused.
Develop a branding style and implement it across the product and marketing to create a cohesive visual identity.
Define the social media communication strategy.

context.title: Pedilo Store
context.body1: Pedilo Store is an online platform designed to simplify WhatsApp order management for small and medium-sized businesses, especially within the restaurant industry.
context.body2: Customers select products from an online catalog, and the merchant receives the organized, summarized order directly on WhatsApp

problem.header: From the perceived problem to the real problem:
problem.left_body: The platform founders set a specific goal: <hl>to acquire more clients.</hl>
problem.right_body1: Digging a little deeper, behind that concrete goal was the concern that <hl>the platform wasn't generating the expected revenue.</hl>
problem.right_body: There was no real clients acquisition problem. The platform already enjoyed organic acquisition of new users. However, of the new registered clients, only a small percentage actively used the platform, and virtually none contributed to its monetization system.
problem.insight_body: The problem wasn't client acquisition; it was a retention problem. And the <hl>objective was financial</hl>.

role.title: Product Chef: discovering what we need to add to the burger.
role.subtitle: · Product Manager & Product Designer ·
role.body: I worked as both the Product Designer and Product Manager, aligning all moving parts of the project—founders, developers (Full Stack and Backend), and Marketing/Social Media—under a single goal. <hl>My mission was to transform a chaotic workflow into a business-driven strategy.</hl>

results.impact1: Design and implementation of the <hl>monetization strategy.</hl>
results.impact3: <hl>Strategic alignment</hl> across all areas: Marketing, Founders, and Development.
results.impact4: Implementation of a <hl>data-driven</hl> culture: tracking metrics.

about.heading:
Designing with a
traveler's curiosity.
about.body1: I'm a Product Designer with 4 years of experience in UX/UI and a Product Management focus on defining roadmaps and prioritizing features.
about.body2: With 10 years in the travel & tourism industry, I integrate that journey to design digital products that are aligned with business goals and deeply centered on the user.
about.body3: As a traveler, my environment changes, but not my routine: I know how to maintain <hl>commitment</hl> and <hl>responsibility</hl> through constant change. I am always in motion, but never stop observing—continually developing the skill to see what goes unnoticed at first glance.
about.body4: I'm a <hl>creative</hl> and a <hl>builder</hl>, bringing a global vision, curiosity, and a drive for constant evolution.
about.metodologia: The journey is also a methodology.

footer.eyebrow: Ready for the next product journey

footer.quote: What is essential is invisible to the eye.
